messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Okay to push a hotfix for an existing release?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Okay to push a hotfix for an existing release?
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I was wondering whether someone from the Bioconductor core team might reply to this e-mail and/or to this GitHub issue [1] to confirm Bioconductor's hotfix policy. Specifically, the web server that JASPAR2024 relies on has changed and the old server SSL certificate expired on 2024-08-31 that breaks the package. It would be great if someone could confirm it's okay to apply the bug fix outside of the typical release cycle to the current release branch. I don't see this hotfix edge case in the general contributor guidelines [2] though of course I may have overlooked it mentioned elsewhere.

Thank you for any help,
Pariksheet

[1]  https://github.com/da-bar/JASPAR2024/issues/3
[2] https://contributions.bioconductor.org/versionnum.html

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Testing the srnadiff package with R CMD check takes lots of time
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Testing the srnadiff package with R CMD check takes lots of time
messages.body_plain:
Dear everyone,


I have difficulties trying to decrease the time spent to check my package, srnadiff (for reproducibility, you can get the GitHub repo from https://github.com/mzytnicki/srnadiff, on the devel branch).


I tried to reduce the tests to 2 files (for reproducibility: test_annotation.R and test_pvalues.R).


When I use devtools::check(), the "checking tests" step takes 29 minutes.  With all the files, it takes more than one hour. Same goes with R CMD check on the built package. On the BioC check platform, it reaches the time limit.


When I use devtools::test(), the same tests takes 8 seconds.


Now, on the computer of my colleague, trying to reproduce the BioC environment variables, the whole devtools::check takes a few minutes.


I am puzzled by the discrepancy, and would like to reduce this time.

Does anyone have a suggestion about how I could proceed?


I used profvis with the command  p <- profvis::profvis({foo <- devtools::check()})

It seems that most of the time is spent on ".Call" (with no other sub-function called), and I cannot interpret this.


I thank you very much for your help.


Best regards,


Matthias.

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] FW: [EXTERNAL] SRAdb problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.21
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I have been trying to fix the following error from the SRAdb building process at Bioconductor servers:

* installing to library ?/home/biocbuild/bbs-3.21-bioc/R/site-library?
* installing *source* package ?SRAdb? ...
** using staged installation
** R
** inst
** byte-compile and prepare package for lazy loading
Error: object ?gunzip? is not exported by 'namespace:GEOquery'
Execution halted
ERROR: lazy loading failed for package ?SRAdb?
* removing ?/home/biocbuild/bbs-3.21-bioc/R/site-library/SRAdb?

Can you give me some suggestions?

Best,

Jack Zhu

Can you give me some suggestions?

Best,

Jack Zhu

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Package check errors Fwd: seqArchR problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.21
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Package check errors Fwd: seqArchR problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.21
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioc team,

I am seeing check errors on nebbiolo1 and palomino7 and build and install
error on kunpeng2 for my package "seqArchR". The check errors are due to
the missing Python module scikit-learn, and others are due to dependencies
not being available. I believe this is being looked into by the team.
Please let me know if you need anything from my side to get this resolved.

Thanks and best wishes,
  Sarvesh


---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: <BBS-noreply at bioconductor.org>
Date: Tue, 3 Dec 2024 at 22:30
Subject: seqArchR problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check
report for BioC 3.21
To: <sarvesh.nikumbh at gmail.com>


[This is an automatically generated email. Please don't reply.]

Hi seqArchR maintainer,

According to the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.21,
the seqArchR package has the following problem(s):

  o ERROR for 'R CMD check' on nebbiolo1. See the details here:

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.21/bioc-LATEST/seqArchR/nebbiolo1-checksrc.html

Please take the time to address this by committing and pushing
changes to your package at git.bioconductor.org

Notes:

  * This was the status of your package at the time this email was sent to
you.
    Given that the online report is updated daily (in normal conditions) you
    could see something different when you visit the URL(s) above,
especially if
    you do so several days after you received this email.

  * It is possible that the problems reported in this report are false
positives,
    either because another package (from CRAN or Bioconductor) breaks your
    package (if yours depends on it) or because of a Build System problem.
    If this is the case, then you can ignore this email.

  * Please check the report again 24h after you've committed your changes
to the
    package and make sure that all the problems have gone.

  * If you have questions about this report or need help with the
    maintenance of your package, please use the Bioc-devel mailing list:

      https://bioconductor.org/help/mailing-list/

    (all package maintainers are requested to subscribe to this list)

For immediate notification of package build status, please
subscribe to your package's RSS feed. Information is at:

https://bioconductor.org/developers/rss-feeds/

Thanks for contributing to the Bioconductor project!



-- 

thanks!
-Sarvesh

--------------------------------------------

A: http://five.sentenc.es

messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I edited the file based on Mike' s suggestion on the git page (image1) but doesn't seem to reflect when I clone the file using the git clone bioconductor package yet. Can someone confirm it will be done and nothing else I have to do from my end.

Let me know.

Regards,
Tulip.



________________________________
From: Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com>
Sent: Tuesday, November 26, 2024 2:50 AM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error


EXTERNAL MAIL

Dear Tulip,

The decideTestsDGE() function was deprecated in edgeR 4.2 and removed in the new 4.4 release.  You can find some notes ion this n the NEWS at https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS__;!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!BCGS40g3d3SSGednQhbxHgpWzGAsYc9EsCe70-JZ9IXVg3a8PO-zA19gV1ChkR4Rh_ekHivdYDmFEO8pn_DJEQx-jd1t$>

It looks  to me like you should be able to use decideTests() as a drop in replacement in your package.

Best,
Mike

messages.body_plain:
You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. Please see the git version control section of the development pages https://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html


-----Original Message-----

I edited the file based on Mike' s suggestion on the git page (image1) but doesn't seem to reflect when I clone the file using the git clone bioconductor package yet. Can someone confirm it will be done and nothing else I have to do from my end.

Let me know.

Regards,
Tulip.

You can find some notes ion this n the NEWS at https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS__;!!K-Hz7m0Vt54!ml36G27n5i71mmZqYh9kGx64fb8MnMWqcgTRb-IOUtzTvcuIr8z00917gwIae3eKB558z662Sqs-Iu35-pnRAO0wmBtwCQ$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS__;!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!BCGS40g3d3SSGednQhbxHgpWzGAsYc9EsCe70-JZ9IXVg3a8PO-zA19gV1ChkR4Rh_ekHivdYDmFEO8pn_DJEQx-jd1t$>

It looks  to me like you should be able to use decideTests() as a drop in replacement in your package.

Best,
Mike

messages.body_plain:
Hi Tulip,

I'm glad you are maintaining groHMM; I previously had not got much response from contributing bug fixes, so you may be interested in looking at and maybe integrating my fork: https://github.com/coregenomics/groHMM where the NEWS file summarizes the changes. There is also a test suite that checks more behaviors than the vignette successfully building.

Pariksheet

You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. Please see the git version control section of the development pages https://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Okay to push a hotfix for an existing release?
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Okay to push a hotfix for an existing release?
messages.body_plain:
Hi Pariksheet,

Thanks for pointing this out.
I have created an issue regarding this scenario here: 
https://github.com/Bioconductor/pkgrevdocs/issues/130
and we will add more details at https://contributions.bioconductor.org

To answer your question, yes, the package should be updated in the 
release and devel branches so that it remains functional.

Best regards,

Marcel

On 12/2/24 10:07 AM, Nanda, Pariksheet via Bioc-devel wrote:
> * This email originates from a sender outside of CUNY. Verify the sender before replying or clicking on links and attachments. *
>
> Hello,
>
> I was wondering whether someone from the Bioconductor core team might reply to this e-mail and/or to this GitHub issue [1] to confirm Bioconductor's hotfix policy. Specifically, the web server that JASPAR2024 relies on has changed and the old server SSL certificate expired on 2024-08-31 that breaks the package. It would be great if someone could confirm it's okay to apply the bug fix outside of the typical release cycle to the current release branch. I don't see this hotfix edge case in the general contributor guidelines [2] though of course I may have overlooked it mentioned elsewhere.
>
> Thank you for any help,
> Pariksheet

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Which remote branch to push changes to
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Which remote branch to push changes to
messages.body_plain:
It depends on if these are bug fixes/vital to package usage or not.  If they are then you should push to both RELEASE_3_20 and devel.

If they are not and are enhancements/new features, then we would highly recommend to only push to the devel branch.

There is no master branch on Bioconductor anymore. The master branch has been renamed to devel.

Cheers,



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error
messages.body_plain:
So the command that fails:

git push origin master

If we break down the command, it loosely is saying push to the remote listed as origin to the branch designation master and I'm on a branch called master (implied)
Looking at your origin remote:
origin git at github.com:Kraus-Lab/groHMM.git

That is to your own or your labs github.  This has nothing to do with Bioconductor permissions but that on the github. You will have to look into how to resolve this on github.

The upstream remote is the Bioconductor location of the package.
upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/groHMM.git

That being said,  what is the name of the branch you are locally on?
Based on the first call, I will assume master.  Bioconductor no longer uses a master branch.  Our default, devel branch is now named devel.  See https://contributions.bioconductor.org/branch-rename-faqs.html
if your local branch is master you would do something like the following to push to git.bioconductor.org

git push upstream master:devel

essentially reading:  push the changes to the remote listed as upstream. And push my local master branch to the remote devel branch.

Cheers,


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. From: Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org>
Sent: Thursday, December 5, 2024 5:50 AM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>; James W. MacDonald <jmacdon at uw.edu>; Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

So the command that fails:

git push origin master

If we break down the command, it loosely is saying push to the remote listed as origin to the branch designation master and I'm on a branch called master (implied)
Looking at your origin remote:
origin git at github.com:Kraus-Lab/groHMM.git

That is to your own or your labs github.  This has nothing to do with Bioconductor permissions but that on the github. You will have to look into how to resolve this on github.

The upstream remote is the Bioconductor location of the package.
upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/groHMM.git

That being said,  what is the name of the branch you are locally on?
Based on the first call, I will assume master.  Bioconductor no longer uses a master branch.  Our default, devel branch is now named devel.  See https://contributions.bioconductor.org/branch-rename-faqs.html<https://secure-web.cisco.com/1Yfvx2WnlOA1-38OJC-f0s4hSAHSe4sZkNnrIKdpH2wM_gJuv27zWUile0Z-RmXGIjoCdoW2MaeZCmK-OsKv1ECgOebY_zY5bBbWswxfeHJwHaCOT0IlkjQsFH-eoDyQsLOjy1ADUuSoMc75wdyphtMTJda7oeISvZfj1_2Wvb2mCv20wupKPdUWtwOfwY3Xum0bfogIjm_6NcgiFOyfHIFTK3LpWcEiBAjz3fqzbk9maKc21qrIJcP2d2YuT5Kb1Jq1sYcL3WzHamPBH8Jh0xd-fLZ-sbD58SZyrx9ydc9GK9NvPD3ukW6z2fDZCCnlo/https%3A%2F%2Furldefense.com%2Fv3%2F__https%3A%2F%2Fcontributions.bioconductor.org%2Fbranch-rename-faqs.html__%3B%21%21MznTZTSvDXGV0Co%21G7xDI5mCtpNfI2_kxFf5jzrWJu0CJwPadHg-eh5tOD5mldEz7G7LeKTWfRVvENVHu8qk4RsBDQ9uu4IYueMoIF41rmTVP6K6Gx5Nz9A%24>
if your local branch is master you would do something like the following to push to git.bioconductor.org

git push upstream master:devel

essentially reading:  push the changes to the remote listed as upstream. And push my local master branch to the remote devel branch.

Cheers,


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Buffalo, New York 14263

From: James W. MacDonald <jmacdon at uw.edu>
Sent: Tuesday, December 3, 2024 2:31 PM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>; Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: RE: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. Please see the git version control section of the development pages https://secure-web.cisco.com/16_v9KvAnSn5NTE4bTiw78EKac1H9ipCVrD15qZr6e0WzggraBQhvfCSIwCLL-NpPpZOxGuzdCoG2QFSZYXUfh02U187DGSNi9GMRoeItGMXmL8Pxv6E1PaofrESKPYKGpKLAAQRczRO1tuXb1oM7q8ahoI5VlJUWZ16K09Ns6efVylSfGwcX3p0elHPzeH8nJ3FihmzlQHBZDYp0wHTwfeGkDBzeGOnqCuaTo92X12THhrzDkwVex8p5FJhW38JXKuMXH9g3J2-jcnq_tqak_SBvoa5WjbL36eOfKKNQpyaUSLEOjiwh6OIg-mYmQ0yx/https%3A%2F%2Furldefense.com%2Fv3%2F__https%3A%2F%2Fcontributions.bioconductor.org%2Fgit-version-control.html__;!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!D1Ec1At0koOWiqp-WDw8kmv5F2XS6u9FgDh9_6bOg6sahspYVpDQH4VMAWnhLzWraEuUptCNTFhuhD3a6ZVoPFJgJ1w$<https://secure-web.cisco.com/1GWLgYnV8zmTyLvVUS6dbRWiraO1NBwG4uVx53Xa1mLCraomRypq5LJ0XEcOwrW3zlkizyb7oAw0gRRC5ZrZISZyvTlsNn0Q_8ld-oxHBg791VEFq3hPjx_gaQyGLutwqq1teoujTwXzTMHkzYp6bqJYTZw8eXvLQGhY78ojU-xIi0Vtdn69ioMDV-w-9v2geDPW7lW09eQdm6YwOXSqgfZ86vdRumg6AqtrRjFYqYXUezvyEaPvWj36dUU51wCmu_mYkR4YJVn9m0khwwGYzRvVkyTyWcen8EUyM8Y9lqj55PmkZsdYEzNEBjaMJ-9xh/https%3A%2F%2Furldefense.com%2Fv3%2F__https%3A%2F%2Fsecure-web.cisco.com%2F16_v9KvAnSn5NTE4bTiw78EKac1H9ipCVrD15qZr6e0WzggraBQhvfCSIwCLL-NpPpZOxGuzdCoG2QFSZYXUfh02U187DGSNi9GMRoeItGMXmL8Pxv6E1PaofrESKPYKGpKLAAQRczRO1tuXb1oM7q8ahoI5VlJUWZ16K09Ns6efVylSfGwcX3p0elHPzeH8nJ3FihmzlQHBZDYp0wHTwfeGkDBzeGOnqCuaTo92X12THhrzDkwVex8p5FJhW38JXKuMXH9g3J2-jcnq_tqak_SBvoa5WjbL36eOfKKNQpyaUSLEOjiwh6OIg-mYmQ0yx%2Fhttps%2A3A%2A2F%2A2Furldefense.com%2A2Fv3%2A2F__https%2A3A%2A2F%2A2Fcontributions.bioconductor.org%2A2Fgit-version-control.html__%3B%21%21MznTZTSvDXGV0Co%21D1Ec1At0koOWiqp-WDw8kmv5F2XS6u9FgDh9_6bOg6sahspYVpDQH4VMAWnhLzWraEuUptCNTFhuhD3a6ZVoPFJgJ1w%24__%3BJSUlJSUlJSUl%21%21MznTZTSvDXGV0Co%21G7xDI5mCtpNfI2_kxFf5jzrWJu0CJwPadHg-eh5tOD5mldEz7G7LeKTWfRVvENVHu8qk4RsBDQ9uu4IYueMoIF41rmTVP6K6zQuhDZQ%24>

Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

So the command that fails:

git push origin master

If we break down the command, it loosely is saying push to the remote listed as origin to the branch designation master and I'm on a branch called master (implied)
Looking at your origin remote:
origin git at github.com:Kraus-Lab/groHMM.git

That is to your own or your labs github.  This has nothing to do with Bioconductor permissions but that on the github. You will have to look into how to resolve this on github.

The upstream remote is the Bioconductor location of the package.
upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/groHMM.git

That being said,  what is the name of the branch you are locally on?
Based on the first call, I will assume master.  Bioconductor no longer uses a master branch.  Our default, devel branch is now named devel.  See https://contributions.bioconductor.org/branch-rename-faqs.html<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://secure-web.cisco.com/1Yfvx2WnlOA1-38OJC-f0s4hSAHSe4sZkNnrIKdpH2wM_gJuv27zWUile0Z-RmXGIjoCdoW2MaeZCmK-OsKv1ECgOebY_zY5bBbWswxfeHJwHaCOT0IlkjQsFH-eoDyQsLOjy1ADUuSoMc75wdyphtMTJda7oeISvZfj1_2Wvb2mCv20wupKPdUWtwOfwY3Xum0bfogIjm_6NcgiFOyfHIFTK3LpWcEiBAjz3fqzbk9maKc21qrIJcP2d2YuT5Kb1Jq1sYcL3WzHamPBH8Jh0xd-fLZ-sbD58SZyrx9ydc9GK9NvPD3ukW6z2fDZCCnlo/https*3A*2F*2Furldefense.com*2Fv3*2F__https*3A*2F*2Fcontributions.bioconductor.org*2Fbranch-rename-faqs.html__*3B*21*21MznTZTSvDXGV0Co*21G7xDI5mCtpNfI2_kxFf5jzrWJu0CJwPadHg-eh5tOD5mldEz7G7LeKTWfRVvENVHu8qk4RsBDQ9uu4IYueMoIF41rmTVP6K6Gx5Nz9A*24__;JSUlJSUlJSUlJSUlJSU!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!EoPGWUxQM15e4Spr8NM9bKEcuR-GNJqeCc6VPg-VHzZ2Jt0T2ZGaDfhU9cv3qIJYnkpZ2YuNzmklafyc5pOda1Lsyo6Nm-B1RSW29Bw$>
if your local branch is master you would do something like the following to push to git.bioconductor.org

git push upstream master:devel

essentially reading:  push the changes to the remote listed as upstream. And push my local master branch to the remote devel branch.

You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. From: Kern, Lori <Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org<mailto:Lori.Shepherd at RoswellPark.org>>
Sent: Thursday, December 5, 2024 5:50 AM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu<mailto:Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>>; James W. MacDonald <jmacdon at uw.edu<mailto:jmacdon at uw.edu>>; Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com<mailto:grimbough at gmail.com>>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org<mailto:bioc-devel at r-project.org> <bioc-devel at r-project.org<mailto:bioc-devel at r-project.org>>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

So the command that fails:

git push origin master

If we break down the command, it loosely is saying push to the remote listed as origin to the branch designation master and I'm on a branch called master (implied)
Looking at your origin remote:
origin git at github.com:Kraus-Lab/groHMM.git<mailto:git at github.com:Kraus-Lab/groHMM.git>

That is to your own or your labs github.  This has nothing to do with Bioconductor permissions but that on the github. You will have to look into how to resolve this on github.

The upstream remote is the Bioconductor location of the package.
upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/groHMM.git<mailto:git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/groHMM.git>

That being said,  what is the name of the branch you are locally on?
Based on the first call, I will assume master.  Bioconductor no longer uses a master branch.  Our default, devel branch is now named devel.  See https://contributions.bioconductor.org/branch-rename-faqs.html<https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/secure-web.cisco.com/1Yfvx2WnlOA1-38OJC-f0s4hSAHSe4sZkNnrIKdpH2wM_gJuv27zWUile0Z-RmXGIjoCdoW2MaeZCmK-OsKv1ECgOebY_zY5bBbWswxfeHJwHaCOT0IlkjQsFH-eoDyQsLOjy1ADUuSoMc75wdyphtMTJda7oeISvZfj1_2Wvb2mCv20wupKPdUWtwOfwY3Xum0bfogIjm_6NcgiFOyfHIFTK3LpWcEiBAjz3fqzbk9maKc21qrIJcP2d2YuT5Kb1Jq1sYcL3WzHamPBH8Jh0xd-fLZ-sbD58SZyrx9ydc9GK9NvPD3ukW6z2fDZCCnlo/https*3A*2F*2Furldefense.com*2Fv3*2F__https*3A*2F*2Fcontributions.bioconductor.org*2Fbranch-rename-faqs.html__*3B*21*21MznTZTSvDXGV0Co*21G7xDI5mCtpNfI2_kxFf5jzrWJu0CJwPadHg-eh5tOD5mldEz7G7LeKTWfRVvENVHu8qk4RsBDQ9uu4IYueMoIF41rmTVP6K6Gx5Nz9A*24__;JSUlJSUlJSUlJSUlJSU!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!EoPGWUxQM15e4Spr8NM9bKEcuR-GNJqeCc6VPg-VHzZ2Jt0T2ZGaDfhU9cv3qIJYnkpZ2YuNzmklafyc5pOda1Lsyo6Nm-B1RSW29Bw$>
if your local branch is master you would do something like the following to push to git.bioconductor.org

git push upstream master:devel

essentially reading:  push the changes to the remote listed as upstream. And push my local master branch to the remote devel branch.

Cheers,


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Buffalo, New York 14263

From: James W. MacDonald <jmacdon at uw.edu<mailto:jmacdon at uw.edu>>

To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu<mailto:Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>>; Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com<mailto:grimbough at gmail.com>>

Subject: RE: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org<mailto:git at git.bioconductor.org>, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org<mailto:git at git.bioconductor.org> set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu<mailto:jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. Dear Tulip,

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Final Reminder: GBCC2025 Logo Design Contest Ends Dec 8!
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Final Reminder: GBCC2025 Logo Design Contest Ends Dec 8!
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

This is a final reminder that the GBCC2025 Logo Contest closes in 2 days, on December 8, 2024.

Don?t miss your chance to design the official logo for the first-ever Galaxy and Bioconductor Community Conference (GBCC2025). The winning design will feature on conference materials and the website, and the top entry will receive a $100 Amazon gift card. Additionally, a $25 Amazon gift card will be drawn randomly from all other entries.

Submit your design here: submission form<https://forms.gle/mZFEhevYU4cccbUv8>.

We?re looking forward to seeing your creative ideas!

Best regards,
Maria


Maria Doyle, PhD
Bioconductor Community Manager

Connect with the Bioconductor community at our conferences:

  *   GBCC 2025 (June 23-26, New York, USA)<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/bioconductor_gbcc2025-bioconductor-galaxy-activity-7236739126190829569-QdEH>
  *   EuroBioC 2025 (September 17-19, Barcelona, Spain)<https://www.linkedin.com/posts/bioconductor_eurobioc2024-eurobioc2025-activity-7237888796564885505-hUW6>



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

will be available two days from now).

Regards,
Martin

> Dear Bioc team,
>
> I am seeing check errors on nebbiolo1 and palomino7 and build and install
> error on kunpeng2 for my package "seqArchR". The check errors are due to
> the missing Python module scikit-learn, and others are due to dependencies
> not being available. I believe this is being looked into by the team.
> Please let me know if you need anything from my side to get this resolved.
>
> Thanks and best wishes,
>   Sarvesh
>
>

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Not able to make changes to repository push to git@git.bioconductor.org
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Not able to make changes to repository push to git@git.bioconductor.org
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,

I am currently listed as an author (role = "aut") for the scHiCcompare
package in its DESCRIPTION file. However, when attempting to push changes
to the repository at:

git remote add upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/scHiCcompare.git
> git remote -v
> git fetch --all
> git push upstream main:devel


Then I got error message:

> "FATAL: W any packages/scHiCcompare mdozmorov DENIED by fallthru
> (or you mis-spelled the reponame)
> fatal: Could not read from remote repository.
> Please make sure you have the correct access rights
> and the repository exists."


I am not too sure how I can have the right to access this.

Thank you,

MN

-- 
My Nguyen (Jasmine)
Graduate Ph.D. Student
Department of Biostatistics
Virginia Commonwealth University School of Population Health

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Not able to make changes to repository push to git@git.bioconductor.org
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Not able to make changes to repository push to git@git.bioconductor.org
messages.body_plain:
Bioconductor will initially only give the maintainer of the package push access.  It is best practice for only one person to have access to push changes; while the other should submit changes to the maintainers repository for merges and review.  If multiple people really need push access,  You will need the maintainer of the package request this. Once requested we can grant additional access;  we will need your BiocCredentials account email/id. If you do not already have a BiocCredentials account, we can assist with creating it.



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

Dear Bioconductor Team,

package in its DESCRIPTION file. However, when attempting to push changes
to the repository at:

git remote add upstream git at git.bioconductor.org:packages/scHiCcompare.git
> git remote -v
> git fetch --all
> git push upstream main:devel


Then I got error message:

> (or you mis-spelled the reponame)
> fatal: Could not read from remote repository.
> Please make sure you have the correct access rights
> and the repository exists."


I am not too sure how I can have the right to access this.

Thank you,

MN

--

Virginia Commonwealth University School of Population Health

From: James W. MacDonald <jmacdon at uw.edu>
Sent: Tuesday, December 3, 2024 2:31 PM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>; Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: RE: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

You need to commit your changes to git at git.bioconductor.org, which you apparently have not done as yet (you should have git at git.bioconductor.org set up as a remote which you can see by doing git remote -v). The last commit is Jennifer Wokaty bumping the version for the last release:

/groHMM$ git log
commit a68457648cdef96ae2a8f40c484496e935d0fcb3 (HEAD -> devel, origin/master, origin/devel, origin/HEAD)
Author: J Wokaty <jennifer.wokaty at sph.cuny.edu>
Date:   Tue Oct 29 09:56:11 2024 -0400

    bump x.y.z version to odd y following creation of RELEASE_3_20 branch

You also need to bump the version number in order to get the new version to build. You also need to make changes to the RELEASE_3_20 branch if you want the fix to propagate to the release version of your package. Please see the git version control section of the development pages https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://contributions.bioconductor.org/git-version-control.html__;!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!D1Ec1At0koOWiqp-WDw8kmv5F2XS6u9FgDh9_6bOg6sahspYVpDQH4VMAWnhLzWraEuUptCNTFhuhD3a6ZVoPFJgJ1w$

Sent: Tuesday, December 3, 2024 3:14 PM

Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error

Let me know.

Regards,
Tulip.

From: Mike Smith <grimbough at gmail.com>
Sent: Tuesday, November 26, 2024 2:50 AM
To: Tulip Nandu <Tulip.Nandu at UTSouthwestern.edu>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] groHMM package error


EXTERNAL MAIL

Dear Tulip,

The decideTestsDGE() function was deprecated in edgeR 4.2 and removed in the new 4.4 release.  You can find some notes ion this n the NEWS at https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS__;!!K-Hz7m0Vt54!ml36G27n5i71mmZqYh9kGx64fb8MnMWqcgTRb-IOUtzTvcuIr8z00917gwIae3eKB558z662Sqs-Iu35-pnRAO0wmBtwCQ$ <https://urldefense.com/v3/__https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/news/edgeR/NEWS__;!!MznTZTSvDXGV0Co!BCGS40g3d3SSGednQhbxHgpWzGAsYc9EsCe70-JZ9IXVg3a8PO-zA19gV1ChkR4Rh_ekHivdYDmFEO8pn_DJEQx-jd1t$>

It looks  to me like you should be able to use decideTests() as a drop in replacement in your package.

Best,
Mike

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.body_plain:
Dear Community,

I submitted a recent version of the package rmspc to Bioconductor a year
ago, to upgrade it. A prerequisite for the rmspc package is .NET 6.0.
Before installing the rmspc package, the program .NET 6.0. should already
be installed on the user's computer.

I recently received an email saying the package didn't pass the platform
build check for the host *nebbiolo2*. I believe this error may have
occurred because .NET 6.0 isn't installed on this platform.

Thank you for your help!

-- 

*Meriem BAHDA*

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] betaHMM problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.21
messages.body_plain:
Thank you for your questions.

Your package is currently failing on both release and devel,  therefore the changes should be pushed to both.  You would need to push to a RELEASE_30 branch to fix release,  and devel branch to fix devel

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Question regarding new ssh key
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Question regarding new ssh key
messages.body_plain:
Hello all,

I recently opened a new issue in Bioconductor Contributions with my package
TrIdent. I need to push updates but realized that I forgot my ssh key
passphrase! Since I am on Windows, there is unfortunately no way to recover
the passphrase. I made a new ssh key and added it to my GitHub. As I
understand it, Bioconductor pulls the key from my GitHub during the initial
moderation step, how can I make sure the new key is pulled so that I can
push changes? Should I delete the previous key?

Best,
Jessie

messages.body_plain:
You can log on directly to the BiocCredentials account to see if it is pulled automatically (as well as add any keys manually).  There is no need to delete the old one unless you want to.



Lori Shepherd - Kern

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

TrIdent. I need to push updates but realized that I forgot my ssh key

push changes? Should I delete the previous key?

Best,
Jessie

messages.body_plain:
I tried running your tests (checked out from github repo at

commit e580d52d7242693b79ec6b8483d68a65d6fb9e02 (HEAD -> master,
origin/master, origin/HEAD)
Author: Matthias Zytnicki <matthias.zytnicki at inra.fr>
Date:   Mon Mar 6 21:35:41 2023 +0100

    version bump

and ran into some serious issues -- a very long stream of

> test_that("Running implemented constructor", {
+     object <- srnadiffExp(bamFiles, sampleInfo, annotReg)
+     expect_is(object, "srnadiffExp")
+  .... [TRUNCATED]
?? Warning: Running implemented constructor
????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????
subscript out of bounds (index 1 >= vector size 1)
Backtrace:
    ?
 1. ??srnadiff::srnadiffExp(bamFiles, sampleInfo, annotReg) at
test_AllClass.R:21:5
 2.   ??srnadiff:::computeNormFactors(object at coverages)
 3.     ??srnadiff:::rcpp_normalization(cvg, librarySize)

?? Warning: Running implemented constructor
????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????????
subscript out of bounds (index 2 >= vector size 1)
Backtrace:
    ?
 1. ??srnadiff::srnadiffExp(bamFiles, sampleInfo, annotReg) at
test_AllClass.R:21:5
 2.   ??srnadiff:::computeNormFactors(object at coverages)
 3.     ??srnadiff:::rcpp_normalization(cvg, librarySize)

Admittedly this is not the right environment to reproduce what you are
seeing but
perhaps it is somewhat helpful.  Have you tried running the tests in
the bioc BBS container?
see https://github.com/bioconductor/bioconductor_salt/pkgs/container/bioconductor_salt

On Mon, Dec 2, 2024 at 3:13?PM Matthias Zytnicki
<matthias.zytnicki at inrae.fr> wrote:
>
> Dear everyone,
>
>
> I have difficulties trying to decrease the time spent to check my package, srnadiff (for reproducibility, you can get the GitHub repo from https://github.com/mzytnicki/srnadiff, on the devel branch).
>
>
> I tried to reduce the tests to 2 files (for reproducibility: test_annotation.R and test_pvalues.R).
>
>
> When I use devtools::check(), the "checking tests" step takes 29 minutes.  With all the files, it takes more than one hour. Same goes with R CMD check on the built package. On the BioC check platform, it reaches the time limit.
>
>
> When I use devtools::test(), the same tests takes 8 seconds.
>
>
> Now, on the computer of my colleague, trying to reproduce the BioC environment variables, the whole devtools::check takes a few minutes.
>
>
> I am puzzled by the discrepancy, and would like to reduce this time.
>
> Does anyone have a suggestion about how I could proceed?
>
>
> I used profvis with the command  p <- profvis::profvis({foo <- devtools::check()})
>
> It seems that most of the time is spent on ".Call" (with no other sub-function called), and I cannot interpret this.
>
>
> I thank you very much for your help.
>
>
> Best regards,
>
>
> Matthias.
>
>         [[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>

Dear Vincent,


I thank you very much for your help.


I never had this "subscript out of bounds" error, and the BioC testing platform either.

Yet, as you suggested, solving this problem (which I still do not exactly know how to reproduce) could maybe solve the other problem as an unexpected but lucky side-effect?


Thanks again,


Matthias.

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

Looking at https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet, support for .NET 6.0 ended November 12, 2024. While .NET 6.0 is available in the Ubuntu backports repository, please consider updating rmspc to work with a currently supported version of .NET.

In the meantime, I've installed .NET 6.0 on our release Linux server and left .NET 8.0 on our devel Linux server, which is available via the Ubuntu repository by default as you may want to consider as a replacement.

Jennifer Wokaty (they/them)

Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
Bioconductor Core Team
________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Kern, Lori via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Tuesday, December 17, 2024 7:07 AM
To: Meriem Bahda <meriembahda at gmail.com>; bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20

* This email originates from a sender outside of CUNY. Verify the sender before replying or clicking on links and attachments. *

Our system administrator is currently out of office. When they return we will look into this.


Lori Shepherd - Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Meriem Bahda <meriembahda at gmail.com>
Sent: Monday, December 16, 2024 8:37 PM
To: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20

Dear Community,

I submitted a recent version of the package rmspc to Bioconductor a year
ago, to upgrade it. A prerequisite for the rmspc package is .NET 6.0.
Before installing the rmspc package, the program .NET 6.0. should already
be installed on the user's computer.

I recently received an email saying the package didn't pass the platform
build check for the host *nebbiolo2*. I believe this error may have
occurred because .NET 6.0 isn't installed on this platform.

Thank you for your help!

--

*Meriem BAHDA*

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]

Subject: [Bioc-devel] rmspc problems reported in the Multiple platform build/check report for BioC 3.20

Dear Community,

I submitted a recent version of the package rmspc to Bioconductor a year
ago, to upgrade it. A prerequisite for the rmspc package is .NET 6.0.
Before installing the rmspc package, the program .NET 6.0. should already
be installed on the user's computer.

I recently received an email saying the package didn't pass the platform
build check for the host *nebbiolo2*. I believe this error may have
occurred because .NET 6.0 isn't installed on this platform.

Thank you for your help!

--

*Meriem BAHDA*

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Fix dependency issues in devel and release
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Fix dependency issues in devel and release
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor Team,

A package that SurfR depended on was recently removed from CRAN. I noticed
that the authors have been active on its GitHub repo lately, so I am
hopeful that it will become available again soon.

In the meantime, I have addressed the issue by rewriting the affected
SurfR-related functions and removing that package from SurfR's
dependencies. Currently, I recommend users who encounter installation
issues due to this dependency to install the development version of SurfR
directly from my GitHub repository.

Would it be possible for you to sync  the released version of SurfR on
Bioconductor to reflect these changes? This would ensure that users can
continue to install and use SurfR without encountering dependency issues.

Thank you for your time and assistance. Please let me know if there are any
additional steps I need to take to facilitate this process.

Best wishes,
Aurora

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.body_plain:
Hi Aurora,

It looks like you were able to sync your package updates to Bioconductor.
I think the question is more about when will those changes be reflected 
in the landing page.

Please note that Bioconductor 3.20 builds every Monday and Thursday [1].

Your fixes should be reflected on today's report (~3 PM EST).


Best regards,

Marcel


PS. You can checked for sync'ed changes at 
https://code.bioconductor.org/browse/SurfR/.

[1]: https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/
